goal: Make the nucleus membrane non-penetrable in Roblox by enabling the imported nucleus collision proxy.
root_cause: Workspace.Nucleus_Model contained 12 anchored visual parts but 0 collidable parts.
The invisible nucleus collision proxy existed under InteractionAnchors, but CanCollide was false.
The proxy was also slightly smaller than the visible outer envelope, letting the avatar overlap the membrane surface before collision.
changes: Added configureNucleusMembraneCollision() to the main builder.
Detects visible nucleus models by name or OrganelleId.
Enables imported collision/proxy parts for the nucleus only.
Sets collision proxies anchored, invisible, queryable, non-touch, and CanCollide=true.
Fits the collision proxy to the visible outer envelope at 103% size so the membrane surface is protected.
Records NucleusMembraneCollisionEnabled, NucleusMembraneCollisionMode, and NucleusMembraneCollisionFit attributes.
validation: Deployed BuildMainCellExperience.lua into Roblox Studio through the legacy bridge.
Ran require(builder).build() and confirmed the live nucleus collision proxy is CanCollide=true.
result: The nucleus membrane now has an invisible collision proxy aligned to the outer envelope, so the player should no longer pass through the nuclear membrane.
